verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Lagide After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Lagide is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.

conversationalSummary.text: Lagide at After work (16:30 start) looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the lack of push could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

best.bestWind: ideal E/ESE/SE, usable NE/ENE/SSE, clean <=25 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 95 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves.  Watch: 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves.
best.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves

photo.alt: Photo of Meio da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 151 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 75/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 112 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 73/100, and wind is the main risk.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 110 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 77/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay
